- M3: All 11 swallowed catch blocks - added Exception logging
- M4: Gemini API key moved from URL query string to x-goog-api-key header
- M5: Rate limiter only trusts X-Forwarded-For from known proxy networks
- M6: Double SaveChanges in session creation wrapped in transaction

Minor fixes:
- Source-generated [GeneratedRegex] in RateLimitMiddleware
- ILogger added to 6 controllers/services
- Structured logging at key session lifecycle events

commit.message:
Fix span attributes lost when Azure Monitor distro sampler drops constructor attrs (#341)

## Problem

When users initialize `AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer` with
`configure_azure_monitor()` (the recommended Azure Monitor distro path),
**all `gen_ai.*` span attributes are silently lost** from Application
Insights. The `customDimensions` field contains only
`_MS.ResourceAttributeId`, and the dependency Type column shows "N/A".

## Root Cause

The OTel Python SDK's `Tracer.start_span()` passes user-provided
`attributes` to the sampler as input, but only copies
`SamplingResult.attributes` onto the created span ([`opentelemetry-sdk
trace/__init__.py` line
1168](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/blob/main/opentelemetry-sdk/src/opentelemetry/sdk/trace/__init__.py#L1168)):

```python
span = _Span(
    ...
    attributes=sampling_result.attributes.copy(),  # Only sampler attrs, NOT user attrs!
)
```

Samplers like the Azure Monitor distro's `RateLimitedSampler` (default
when using `configure_azure_monitor()`) do not return user attributes in
their `SamplingResult`. Attributes set via `span.set_attribute()` after
creation work fine because they write directly to `span._attributes`.

## Evidence

| Setup | `customDimensions` has `gen_ai.*` | Type column |
|-------|-----------------------------------|-------------|
| `configure_azure_monitor()` (distro) | ❌ Empty | N/A |
| Manual `TracerProvider` + `AzureMonitorTraceExporter` | ✅ All present
| azure_openai |
| `configure_azure_monitor()` + `OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER=always_on` | ✅ All
present | azure_openai |

## Fix

After `start_span()`, explicitly re-apply all attributes via
`set_attribute()`. The attributes are still passed to `start_span()` for
sampler input, but the `set_attribute()` calls guarantee they survive
regardless of sampler implementation.

## Affected Attributes

- `gen_ai.operation.name` (invoke_agent, chat, execute_tool)
- `gen_ai.provider.name` (shows "N/A" in Azure Monitor Type column)
- `gen_ai.agent.name` / `gen_ai.agent.id`
- `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens` / `output_tokens`
- `gen_ai.request.model` / `gen_ai.response.model`
- All other attributes passed to `_start_span()`

## Test

Added
`test_start_span_attributes_survive_sampler_that_drops_constructor_attrs`
with a `DroppingMockTracer` that simulates a sampler discarding
constructor attributes. All 96 existing tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

len(frame.layout.channels) instead. Patch activates only when

commit.message:
Fix OOM crash: guard all f64-to-usize casts used for allocation sizes

Add safe conversion helpers clamped_f64_to_usize() and
checked_f64_to_length() in builtins::util that prevent corrupt f64
values (like 0x1c000000000000 = 7168 TiB) from becoming allocation
sizes. Apply them across all code paths where a JS-visible numeric
value is converted to a Rust usize for Vec::with_capacity, vec![],
String::with_capacity, arraybuffer_new, typed_array constructors,
DataView getters/setters, SharedArrayBuffer, Atomics, and string
operations (charAt, substring, codePointAt, toFixed, padStart/padEnd).

Previously the Test262 CI would crash with 'memory allocation of
7881299347898368 bytes failed' around test 4000 of 53321. The specific
bit pattern 0x1c000000000000 was being cast directly from f64 to
usize without bounds checking.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(tariffzones): replace start/end with hour-list zones (up to 3)

Replace zone_1_start/zone_1_end config parameters with explicit
hour-list assignment per zone:

Config parameters:
- zone_1_hours: comma-separated hours for zone 1 (e.g. 7,8,9,...,22)
- zone_2_hours: comma-separated hours for zone 2 (e.g. 0,1,...,6,23)
- zone_3_hours: optional third zone hours
- tariff_zone_3: price for optional third zone

Validation rules (all enforced at price-generation time):
- No hour may appear in more than one zone (ValueError)
- No duplicate within a single zone (ValueError)
- All 24 hours 0-23 must be covered (ValueError)
- zone_3_hours and tariff_zone_3 must both be set or both omitted

Input formats accepted for *_hours: comma-separated string (as YAML
provides), Python list/tuple, or single integer.

Updated:
- TariffZones: new _parse_hours(), _validate_configuration(), properties
- DynamicTariff factory: requires zone_1_hours + zone_2_hours
- batcontrol_config_dummy.yaml: updated example comments
- Tests: 24 tests covering parse, validation, 2-zone, 3-zone, factory

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(tariffzones): add range syntax support for zone hours

_parse_hours now accepts inclusive range tokens (e.g. '0-5') in
addition to single values, enabling compact config like:

  zone_1_hours: 7-22
  zone_2_hours: 0-6,23
  zone_3_hours: 17-20

Ranges are expanded inclusively (7-22 → [7,8,...,22]).
Single integers and range strings may be freely mixed.
List/tuple elements that are Python ints are handled directly
(no string conversion) to avoid treating negative integers as ranges.

Validation unchanged: inverted ranges (5-3) and out-of-bounds
values raise ValueError.

Updated config dummy example to use range notation.
Added 7 new parse_hours tests covering ranges.

Fix UI anchoring and remove unsupported emoji

GameSetupEditor: replace broken position-based anchor heuristic in
CreateUIText with GetAnchorFromTextAnchor() that derives anchor/pivot
directly from the TextAnchor enum. The old code checked position.y >= 0
to detect 'top' elements, but all top-aligned elements have negative y
(offset downward from top anchor), so everything got default center anchors
and appeared in the middle of the screen.

WaveManager: replace ⚠ (U+26A0) with ASCII '[!!]' — LiberationSans SDF
does not include this glyph and logged a warning every wave announcement.

Re-run DuckRevolution > Setup Game to regenerate scene with correct layout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(wayland): implement full event reading and dispatch infrastructure

Completes TD-4 by adding wire protocol event parsing and object event handling.

Changes:
- Add ReadMessage() to Connection for reading compositor events (27 LOC)
- Add DispatchMessage() to route events to registered handlers (15 LOC)
- Implement EventHandler interface for event-capable objects
- Add HandleEvent() for Keyboard, Pointer, Touch, Seat input objects (485 LOC)
- Update processWaylandEvents() to read and dispatch inbound messages
- Handle all major Wayland input events (keyboard, mouse, touch)

This enables interactive Wayland applications to receive user input and
compositor notifications.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
network: Fix sync loop spam and coordinator throttle bypass

Two related log-spam and efficiency fixes:

1. Rate-limit VRF proposer log and Only-N-peers warning to once per 30s when >=5 blocks behind. Previously fired every second, producing ~7 MB/day of log spam.

2. spawn_sync_coordinator now gates through sync_coordinator.request_sync() so the 60s per-peer throttle is respected on the catch-up path.
